title: ficaria verna subsp. ledebourii
intro: Ficaria verna subsp. ledebourii, also known as the lesser celandine or ledebour's celandine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Ficaria verna subsp. ledebourii has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. Ficaria verna subsp. ledebourii can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in a range of herbal preparations.
